text: The video displays the in situ colorectal anastomosis following the resection of rectal cancer, showing the surgical site where the bowel segments have been joined.
The surgeon administers five milliliters of indocyanine green via a peripheral cannula. A near-infrared laparoscope is used through a proctoscope to visualize the colorectal anastomosis internally.
A second near infrared laparoscope provides a view of the colorectal anastomosis from the abdominal side, highlighting the surgical site with fluorescence imaging.
The fluorescence endoscopy reveals excellent perfusion of the colorectal anastomosis, with visible fluorescence both proximal and distal to the circular stapled anastomosis.
The laparoscopic view shows excellent serosal perfusion on the serosal aspect of the bowel, confirmed by the second laparoscopic system during the operation.
